title: Slow Braised Venezuelan Pork Pasta
description: A hearty and flavorful Venezuelan dish featuring slow braised pork and pappardelle pasta, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This sugar-free-friendly recipe is cooked to perfection in a tagine pot.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the pork shoulder with cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in the tagine pot over medium heat and sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped onion to the tagine pot and cook until softened.
Add the minced garlic to the tagine pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth to the tagine pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the tagine pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
Braise the pork shoulder for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender and easily shredded.
Cook the pappardelle pasta according to package instructions.
Shred the pork shoulder and serve over the cooked pappardelle pasta.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
